steps.canonical_text: Do not enter the water unless you are trained. Try to reach with a long object, throw a flotation device, or row a boat to them.
Call emergency services now. Call even if they seem fine after rescue, since lung problems can appear later.
Once they are on a firm flat surface, check if they respond and whether they are breathing normally. Treat occasional gasps as not breathing.
Do not press on the abdomen or hold them upside down to drain water. This delays care and can cause vomiting.
must_keep_keywords: Do not press on the abdomen

steps.canonical_text: If they are not breathing, start chest compressions in the center of the chest. If you are trained, give 5 initial rescue breaths and continue with 30 compressions and 2 breaths.
If the water was cold, also remove wet clothing once they are stable, dry them, and cover with blankets to prevent hypothermia.
